# semctl command reference

<!-- Generated by `go generate ./internal/commands/...`; do not edit by hand. -->

Semaphore UI CLI

## Global flags

These flags are accepted by every command:

```
      --debug             Debug output
      --host string       Semaphore UI host URL
      --json              Output in JSON format
      --no-color          Disable colored output
      --no-interactive    Disable interactive prompts
  -o, --output string     Output format (table, json, yaml, text)
      --profile string    Configuration profile
  -p, --project string    Default project
      --rich-exit-codes   Exit with the error class's exit code instead of 1 (also via SEMCTL_RICH_EXIT)
      --verbose           Verbose output
```

## semctl api

Make an authenticated API request

Make a raw authenticated request to the Semaphore UI API.

The path is relative to /api. Use --field for typed JSON values and --raw-field
for string values. Use --input to send a JSON body from a file or stdin.

This is the escape hatch for endpoints not yet covered by first-class commands.

Usage:

```
semctl api <METHOD> <PATH> [flags]
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl api GET /info
semctl api GET /projects
semctl api POST /project/1/tasks --field template_id=7
semctl api GET /project/1/tasks/last
echo '{"message":"deploy"}' | semctl api POST /project/1/tasks --input -
```

Flags:

```
  -F, --field stringArray       Typed request field
  -H, --header stringArray      HTTP header
      --input string            Read request body from file or stdin
  -f, --raw-field stringArray   String request field
```

## semctl auth

Authenticate with Semaphore UI

Manage authentication state and credentials.

Usage:

```
semctl auth
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl auth login https://semaphore.example.com
pass show semaphore/token | semctl auth login https://semaphore.example.com --with-token
semctl auth status
semctl auth logout
```

## semctl auth login

Authenticate to a Semaphore UI instance

Log in to a Semaphore UI server with an API token or username/password.

Interactive mode prompts for a token with hidden input. Use --with-token to pipe
a token from stdin (e.g., from a password manager). Tokens are stored in the OS
keyring when possible; use --plaintext only if the keyring is unavailable.

Use --cookie to authenticate with username/password instead of an API token.
This stores a session cookie for subsequent requests.

The host is required and must be an absolute URL (https:// or http://).

Usage:

```
semctl auth login [HOST] [flags]
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl auth login https://semaphore.example.com
echo "$TOKEN" | semctl auth login https://semaphore.example.com --with-token
SEMAPHORE_HOST=https://semaphore.example.com semctl auth login --with-token
semctl auth login https://semaphore.example.com --cookie
semctl auth login https://semaphore.example.com --cookie --username admin --password changeme
```

Flags:

```
      --cookie            Authenticate with username/password cookie session
      --password string   Password for cookie authentication (also SEMAPHORE_PASSWORD env)
      --plaintext         Allow storing token in config file if keyring is unavailable
      --username string   Username for cookie authentication (also SEMAPHORE_USERNAME env)
      --with-token        Read token from stdin
```

## semctl auth logout

Remove authentication for a host

Remove the stored token for a host from the OS keyring and clear it from the active profile.

Usage:

```
semctl auth logout [HOST]
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl auth logout https://semaphore.example.com
semctl auth logout
```

## semctl auth status

View authentication status

Show the current host, profile, and whether the stored token is valid.

Usage:

```
semctl auth status
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl auth status
```

## semctl config

Manage configuration

Inspect and edit local CLI configuration, including profiles and defaults.

Usage:

```
semctl config
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl config get host
semctl config set output json
semctl config list
```

## semctl config get

Get a configuration value

Read a single value from the active profile in the config file.

Usage:

```
semctl config get <KEY>
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl config get host
semctl config get current_profile
```

## semctl config list

List configuration values

Show the current profile and key settings from the config file.

Usage:

```
semctl config list
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl config list
```

## semctl config profile

Manage profiles

Create, switch between, and remove named profiles that store host and project defaults.

Usage:

```
semctl config profile
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl config profile list
semctl config profile use prod
semctl config profile create lab --host https://lab.example.com
```

## semctl config profile create

Create a new profile

Create a profile with an optional host URL. Use 'semctl config set host <url>' afterwards if --host is omitted.

Usage:

```
semctl config profile create <NAME> [flags]
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl config profile create prod --host https://semaphore.example.com
semctl config profile create lab
```

Flags:

```
      --host string   Semaphore UI host URL
```

## semctl config profile delete

Delete a profile

Remove a profile from the config file. If it is the active profile, the active profile is cleared.

Usage:

```
semctl config profile delete <NAME>
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl config profile delete lab
```

## semctl config profile list

List profiles

Show all configured profiles and mark the active one with an asterisk.

Usage:

```
semctl config profile list
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl config profile list
```

## semctl config profile use

Switch to a profile

Change the active profile used for all subsequent commands.

Usage:

```
semctl config profile use <NAME>
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl config profile use prod
```

## semctl config set

Set a configuration value

Write a value to the active profile in the config file.

Usage:

```
semctl config set <KEY> <VALUE>
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl config set output json
semctl config set project infra
```

## semctl environment

Manage environments

List, inspect, create, update, and delete environments within the active project.

Usage:

```
semctl environment
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl environment list
semctl environment get staging-env
semctl environment create --name staging-env --json '{"KEY":"value"}'
semctl environment update staging-env --json '{"KEY":"new"}'
semctl environment delete staging-env
```

## semctl environment create

Create an environment

Create a new environment in the active project.

Usage:

```
semctl environment create [flags]
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl environment create --name staging-env
semctl environment create --name staging-env --json '{"KEY":"value"}'
```

Flags:

```
      --json string   Environment variables as a JSON string (default "{}")
      --name string   Environment name
```

## semctl environment delete

Delete an environment

Delete an environment. Accepts an environment ID or name.

Usage:

```
semctl environment delete <ENVIRONMENT>
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl environment delete staging-env
semctl environment delete 5
```

## semctl environment get

Get environment details

Show full details for an environment. Accepts an environment ID or name.

Usage:

```
semctl environment get <ENVIRONMENT>
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl environment get staging-env
semctl environment get 5 --output yaml
```

## semctl environment list

List environments

Show all environments in the active project.

Usage:

```
semctl environment list [flags]
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl environment list
semctl environment list --json
semctl environment list --limit 20 --page 2
```

Flags:

```
      --limit int   Maximum number of items to return
      --page int    Page number to retrieve (1-based)
```

## semctl environment update

Update an environment

Update an environment. Accepts an environment ID or name. Only changed fields are sent.

Usage:

```
semctl environment update <ENVIRONMENT> [flags]
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl environment update staging-env --json '{"KEY":"new"}'
semctl environment update 5 --name prod-env
```

Flags:

```
      --json string   Environment variables as a JSON string (default "{}")
      --name string   Environment name
```

## semctl info

Show server information

Display version and health information from the Semaphore UI server.

Usage:

```
semctl info
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl info
semctl info --host https://semaphore.example.com
```

## semctl inventory

Manage inventories

List, inspect, create, update, and delete inventories within the active project.

Usage:

```
semctl inventory
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl inventory list
semctl inventory get prod-hosts
semctl inventory create --name prod-hosts --inventory-file hosts.ini
semctl inventory update prod-hosts --type file
semctl inventory delete prod-hosts
```

## semctl inventory create

Create an inventory

Create a new inventory in the active project.

Usage:

```
semctl inventory create [flags]
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl inventory create --name prod-hosts --inventory-file hosts.ini
semctl inventory create --name dynamic --type file
```

Flags:

```
      --become-key-id string    Become key (login_password keystore) ID; 'null' to unset
      --inventory string        Inventory content (\n, \t, \r escapes are interpreted)
      --inventory-file string   Read inventory content from a file
      --name string             Inventory name
      --ssh-key-id string       SSH key (keystore) ID; 'null' to unset
      --type string             Inventory type (static, file, etc.) (default "static")
```

## semctl inventory delete

Delete an inventory

Delete an inventory. Accepts an inventory ID or name.

Usage:

```
semctl inventory delete <INVENTORY>
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl inventory delete prod-hosts
semctl inventory delete 3
```

## semctl inventory get

Get inventory details

Show full details for an inventory. Accepts an inventory ID or name.

Usage:

```
semctl inventory get <INVENTORY>
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl inventory get prod-hosts
semctl inventory get 3 --output yaml
```

## semctl inventory list

List inventories

Show all inventories in the active project.

Usage:

```
semctl inventory list [flags]
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl inventory list
semctl inventory list --json
semctl inventory list --limit 20 --page 2
```

Flags:

```
      --limit int   Maximum number of items to return
      --page int    Page number to retrieve (1-based)
```

## semctl inventory update

Update an inventory

Update an inventory. Accepts an inventory ID or name. Only changed fields are sent.

Usage:

```
semctl inventory update <INVENTORY> [flags]
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl inventory update prod-hosts --type file
semctl inventory update 3 --inventory-file hosts.ini
```

Flags:

```
      --become-key-id string    Become key (login_password keystore) ID; 'null' to unset
      --inventory string        Inventory content (\n, \t, \r escapes are interpreted)
      --inventory-file string   Read inventory content from a file
      --name string             Inventory name
      --ssh-key-id string       SSH key (keystore) ID; 'null' to unset
      --type string             Inventory type (static, file, etc.) (default "static")
```

## semctl keystore

Manage keystore / access keys

List, inspect, create, update, and delete access keys within the active project.

Usage:

```
semctl keystore
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl keystore list
semctl keystore get deploy-key
semctl keystore create --name deploy-key --type ssh --login git --private-key-file id_ed25519
semctl keystore update deploy-key --login deploy
semctl keystore delete deploy-key
```

## semctl keystore create

Create an access key

Create a new access key in the active project.

Usage:

```
semctl keystore create [flags]
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl keystore create --name deploy-key --type ssh --login git --private-key-file id_ed25519
semctl keystore create --name vault-pass --type login_password --login admin --password s3cret
```

Flags:

```
      --login string              Login / username
      --name string               Key name
      --password string           Password (login_password type)
      --private-key string        SSH private key contents (ssh type)
      --private-key-file string   Read SSH private key from a file (ssh type)
      --type string               Key type (ssh, login_password, none) (default "none")
```

## semctl keystore delete

Delete an access key

Delete an access key. Accepts a keystore ID or name.

Usage:

```
semctl keystore delete <KEYSTORE>
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl keystore delete deploy-key
semctl keystore delete 9
```

## semctl keystore get

Get keystore details

Show full details for an access key. Accepts a keystore ID or name.

Usage:

```
semctl keystore get <KEYSTORE>
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl keystore get deploy-key
semctl keystore get 9 --output yaml
```

## semctl keystore list

List keystores

Show all access keys in the active project.

Usage:

```
semctl keystore list
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl keystore list
semctl keystore list --json
```

## semctl keystore update

Update an access key

Update an access key. Accepts a keystore ID or name. Only changed fields are sent.

Usage:

```
semctl keystore update <KEYSTORE> [flags]
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl keystore update deploy-key --login deploy
semctl keystore update 9 --type login_password --login admin --password s3cret
```

Flags:

```
      --login string              Login / username
      --name string               Key name
      --password string           Password (login_password type)
      --private-key string        SSH private key contents (ssh type)
      --private-key-file string   Read SSH private key from a file (ssh type)
      --type string               Key type (ssh, login_password, none) (default "none")
```

## semctl ping

Check connectivity to Semaphore UI

Send a lightweight request to verify that the configured host is reachable.

Usage:

```
semctl ping
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl ping
semctl ping --host https://semaphore.example.com
```

## semctl project

Manage projects

List projects, inspect details, and set the default project for the active profile.

Usage:

```
semctl project
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl project list
semctl project get infra
semctl project use infra
```

## semctl project create

Create a project

Create a new project on the current host.

Usage:

```
semctl project create [flags]
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl project create --name infra
semctl project create --name infra --max-parallel 10
```

Flags:

```
      --max-parallel int   Maximum parallel tasks
      --name string        Project name
```

## semctl project delete

Delete a project

Delete a project. Accepts a project ID or name.

Usage:

```
semctl project delete <PROJECT>
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl project delete infra
semctl project delete 1
```

## semctl project get

Get project details

Show full details for a project. Accepts a project ID or name.

Usage:

```
semctl project get <PROJECT>
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl project get infra
semctl project get 1 --output yaml
```

## semctl project list

List projects

Show all projects accessible on the current host.

Usage:

```
semctl project list
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl project list
semctl project list --json
```

## semctl project set

Set the default project for a profile

Store the default project name in the active profile so commands that require a project use it automatically. Also supports updating host and output.

Usage:

```
semctl project set <PROJECT> [flags]
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl project set infra
semctl project set infra --host https://semaphore.example.com --output json
```

Flags:

```
      --host string     Update profile host
  -o, --output string   Update profile default output
```

## semctl project use

Set the default project for a profile

Store the default project name in the active profile so commands that require a project use it automatically.

Usage:

```
semctl project use <PROJECT>
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl project use infra
```

## semctl schedule

Manage schedules

List, inspect, create, update, and delete cron schedules within the active project.

Usage:

```
semctl schedule
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl schedule list
semctl schedule get nightly-deploy
semctl schedule create --template deploy-prod --cron "0 2 * * *" --name nightly-deploy
```

## semctl schedule create

Create a schedule

Create a new cron schedule for a template in the active project.

Usage:

```
semctl schedule create [flags]
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl schedule create --template deploy-prod --cron "0 2 * * *"
semctl schedule create --template 7 --cron "0 2 * * *" --name nightly-deploy
```

Flags:

```
      --active            Whether the schedule is enabled (default true)
      --cron string       Cron expression (required)
      --name string       Schedule name
      --template string   Template ID or name (required)
```

## semctl schedule delete

Delete a schedule

Delete a schedule. Accepts a schedule ID or name.

Usage:

```
semctl schedule delete <SCHEDULE>
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl schedule delete nightly-deploy
semctl schedule delete 3
```

## semctl schedule get

Get schedule details

Show full details for a schedule. Accepts a schedule ID or name.

Usage:

```
semctl schedule get <SCHEDULE>
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl schedule get nightly-deploy
semctl schedule get 3 --output yaml
```

## semctl schedule list

List schedules

Show all schedules in the active project.

Usage:

```
semctl schedule list [flags]
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl schedule list
semctl schedule list --json
semctl schedule list --limit 20 --page 2
```

Flags:

```
      --limit int   Maximum number of items to return
      --page int    Page number to retrieve (1-based)
```

## semctl schedule update

Update a schedule

Update an existing schedule. Accepts a schedule ID or name.

Usage:

```
semctl schedule update <SCHEDULE> [flags]
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl schedule update nightly-deploy --cron "0 3 * * *"
semctl schedule update 3 --name weekly-deploy --template deploy-prod
```

Flags:

```
      --active            Whether the schedule is enabled (default true)
      --cron string       Cron expression
      --name string       Schedule name
      --template string   Template ID or name
```

## semctl task

Manage tasks

List, inspect, run, stop, and watch tasks. Tasks are the core operational workflow in Semaphore UI.

Usage:

```
semctl task
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl task list
semctl task run deploy-prod --message "Deploy 1.8.3" --watch --exit-code
semctl task logs --follow
semctl task stop 812
```

## semctl task get

Get task details

Show full details for a task. Accepts a task ID or name.

Usage:

```
semctl task get <TASK>
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl task get 812
semctl task get 812 --output yaml
```

## semctl task last

Show the latest task

Show details for the most recently created task in the active project.

Usage:

```
semctl task last
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl task last
semctl task last --output yaml
```

## semctl task list

List tasks

Show tasks in the active project.

Usage:

```
semctl task list [flags]
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl task list
semctl task list --json
semctl task list --limit 20 --page 2
```

Flags:

```
      --limit int   Maximum number of items to return
      --page int    Page number to retrieve (1-based)
```

## semctl task logs

View task logs

Print task output. If no task is given, uses the latest task in the active project.

In follow mode, new output is polled and printed until the task finishes.
ANSI escape sequences are stripped by default in TTY mode to prevent malicious
playbooks from hiding text or altering the terminal; use --escape-sanitize=false
to preserve them.

Usage:

```
semctl task logs [TASK] [flags]
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl task logs 812
semctl task logs --follow
semctl task logs 812 --tail 50 --raw
semctl task logs 812 --follow --interval 5s
```

Flags:

```
      --escape-sanitize     Strip ANSI escape sequences from task output (default true)
  -f, --follow              Stream new output
      --interval duration   Polling interval (default 2s)
      --raw                 Skip formatting
      --tail int            Limit initial output lines
```

## semctl task run

Run a template

Queue a new task from a template in the active project.

Use --watch to block until the task finishes. With --exit-code, the CLI returns
a status-specific exit code suitable for CI pipelines:

  0  task succeeded
  1  task failed
  2  task stopped or canceled
  3  timeout
  4  CLI or API error

Ansible execution can be tuned with --tags, --skip-tags, --extra-vars, and
--check. --check enables Ansible check mode (a dry run on the target hosts),
which is distinct from --dry-run.

Usage:

```
semctl task run <TEMPLATE> [flags]
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl task run deploy-prod --message "Deploy release 1.8.3"
semctl task run deploy-prod --watch --exit-code
semctl task run deploy-prod --message "hotfix" --branch release/1.8
semctl task run deploy-prod --environment 1 --inventory 2 --limit "web*" --diff --dry-run
semctl task run deploy-prod --tags deploy,restart --skip-tags slow
semctl task run deploy-prod --extra-vars '{"version":"1.2.3"}' --check
```

Flags:

```
      --branch string        Git branch override
      --check                Ansible check mode (dry run on target hosts; distinct from --dry-run)
      --diff                 Show diff mode
      --dry-run              Dry run mode
      --environment string   Environment ID or name
      --exit-code            Return task status as process exit code
      --extra-vars string    Ansible extra variables as a raw JSON object (e.g. '{"version":"1.2.3"}')
      --inventory string     Inventory ID or name
      --limit string         Ansible limit pattern
      --message string       Task message
      --skip-tags string     Ansible tags to skip (comma-separated)
      --tags string          Ansible tags to run (comma-separated)
      --watch                Wait for the task to complete
```

## semctl task stop

Stop a running task

Request that a running or pending task be stopped. Accepts a task ID.

By default this performs a graceful stop (the task moves to "stopping" and is
finalized once its runner reports back). A waiting/queued task that no runner
has picked up will not transition this way — use --force to mark it stopped
immediately, matching the "Force Stop" action in the Semaphore UI.

The stop request returns as soon as Semaphore accepts it. Use --wait to poll
until the task actually reaches a terminal state, bounded by --timeout
(default 5m; 0 waits indefinitely). With --exit-code the process exit status
reflects the task's final state:

  0  task finished successfully (before the stop took effect)
  1  task failed
  2  task stopped or canceled (the expected result of a stop)
  3  timed out waiting
  4  CLI or API error

Usage:

```
semctl task stop <TASK> [flags]
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl task stop 812
semctl task stop 812 --force
semctl task stop 812 --force --wait --timeout 60s
```

Flags:

```
      --exit-code           with --wait, return the task's final state as the process exit code
      --force               force the task to stop immediately (required for queued/waiting tasks)
      --interval duration   with --wait, polling interval (default 2s)
      --timeout duration    with --wait, give up after this duration (0 = wait indefinitely) (default 5m0s)
      --wait                poll until the task reaches a terminal state after the stop is requested
```

## semctl task watch

Wait for a task to complete

Poll a task until it reaches a terminal state (success, error, or stopped).

If no task is given, watches the latest task in the active project.
With --exit-code, the CLI exits with a status-specific code:

  0  task succeeded
  1  task failed
  2  task stopped or canceled
  3  timeout / context cancelled
  4  CLI or API error

Usage:

```
semctl task watch [TASK] [flags]
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl task watch 812
semctl task watch --exit-code
semctl task run deploy-prod --watch --exit-code
```

Flags:

```
      --exit-code   Return task status as process exit code
```

## semctl template

Manage templates

List and inspect task templates within the active project.

Usage:

```
semctl template
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl template list
semctl template get deploy-prod
```

## semctl template clone

Clone a template

Clone an existing task template with a new name. Accepts a template ID or name.

Usage:

```
semctl template clone <TEMPLATE> <NEW_NAME>
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl template clone deploy-prod deploy-staging
semctl template clone 7 deploy-staging
```

## semctl template delete

Delete a template

Delete a task template. Accepts a template ID or name.

Usage:

```
semctl template delete <TEMPLATE>
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl template delete deploy-prod
semctl template delete 7
```

## semctl template get

Get template details

Show full details for a template. Accepts a template ID or name.

Usage:

```
semctl template get <TEMPLATE>
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl template get deploy-prod
semctl template get 7 --output yaml
```

## semctl template list

List templates

Show all task templates in the active project.

Usage:

```
semctl template list [flags]
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl template list
semctl template list --json
semctl template list --limit 20 --page 2
```

Flags:

```
      --limit int   Maximum number of items to return
      --page int    Page number to retrieve (1-based)
```

## semctl template tasks

List tasks for a template

Show tasks associated with a template. Accepts a template ID or name.

Usage:

```
semctl template tasks <TEMPLATE> [flags]
```

Examples:

```bash
semctl template tasks deploy-prod
semctl template tasks 7 --limit 10
```

Flags:

```
      --limit int   Maximum number of items to return
      --page int    Page number to retrieve (1-based)
```
